prompt: Give me a short pep talk before I start a hard task I have been putting off all morning.
response:
Alright, here we go! You've got this! This challenge might seem big and scary, but that's just what it wants you to think. You've faced tough tasks before and come out victorious. Remember when you took on [insert past challenge here]? How did you feel after you crushed it? 

You're stronger, smarter, and more capable than this task. It's not your job to be perfect, it's your job to do your best. So take a deep breath and remind yourself of all your successes. Visualize the great feeling of accomplishment you'll have when you're done. Trust yourself. You're about to tackle this and triumph! You've built yourself so much strength and resilience through facing previous challenges, so don't you dare doubt yourself now. It might be hard, but it's not impossible and it certainly isn't better than you. 

You've been putting this off because you worked up a fear of the unknown. But the unknown isn't as bad as what's running through your mind right now. The anticipation is what's really eating at you, isn't it? The fear of potentially not doing it to the best of your ability. Fear is natural, it's okay. But don't let it decide your story for you. 

You are a problem solver. This is a problem and you're going to solve it. You're not just going to solve it, you're going to resolve it. Reserve your energy for the task at hand, don't waste it by worrying about "what ifs". You're a capable individual with a whole lot of potential. Believe in yourself because you deserve it. You're worthy of feeling strength and the ability to tackle anything in your way.

Let's do this - you've got this! Embrace the challenge and earn that amazing feeling of pride and accomplishment. Recall all the challenges you've already beaten. Your strength lies not only in your capability, but in your resilience. You have come out the other side in every single challenge thrown at you. Here's another opportunity to reflect on your personal strength.

Tune out the voice of self-doubt. You don't want it anywhere near you right now. You're about to eat this task for breakfast and I'm about to watch you do it. Remember the sense of pride you'll have after this. You will look back at your past self who almost gave up, with your head held high. 

Alright, let's dive in. You're ready!
